title: betula pubescens subsp. murithii
intro: Betula pubescens subsp. murithii, also known as the downy birch, is a subspecies of the birch tree that belongs to the Betulaceae family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The bark and leaves of the downy birch have been traditionally used to treat conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and skin infections due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of downy birch include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
